commands.review_summary:
The branch recomputes known-priced recorded-zero session costs, updates session-log display, bumps the usage-cost cache version, and adds DeepSeek/session-cost regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +240. Total +264 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows a nonzero-token DeepSeek V4 row with usage.cost.total 0 is preserved as zero, and the new cross-provider regression is source-reproducible through OpenRouter total_cost 0 plus known pricing.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can narrow the recorded-zero predicate and add cross-provider regression coverage; maintainer canonical-branch selection remains separate before landing.

target.title: fix(deepseek): recover V4 zero-cost spend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97047.

DeepSeek V4 usage can still surface as zero spend when session transcripts contain nonzero token usage with `usage.cost.total: 0` even though the model has known positive pricing. That leaves Control UI Spend and session log views reporting known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as zero.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change keeps the generic session-cost guard from preserving recorded zero totals for known-priced nonzero-token usage, clears stale cost breakdowns so aggregation uses the recomputed estimate, and bumps the usage-cost cache version so older cached zero totals are rebuilt.

It also keeps DeepSeek provider policy compatibility-safe: missing DeepSeek model `cost` metadata hydrates from the bundled catalog, but explicit all-zero `cost` overrides are preserved rather than silently replaced with catalog pricing.

## User Impact

DeepSeek V4 spend summaries, cache rebuilds, and session logs now show estimated cost for known-priced token usage instead of a fabricated zero.

Configured DeepSeek models without a `cost` block still recover bundled catalog pricing. Configured DeepSeek models with an explicit all-zero `cost` block remain zero-cost overrides.

## Review Items Addressed

- Fixed the `check:test-types` issue by using the cache summary API's typed `startMs`/`endMs` parameters.
- Fixed the session-log fixture so the transcript produces a returned log entry.
- Removed the direct `CHANGELOG.md` edit; release-note context now stays in this PR body only.
- Added redacted after-fix behavior proof for the known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero-cost path.
- Addressed the ClawSweeper P1 compatibility finding by preserving explicit all-zero DeepSeek cost overrides and narrowing the provider-policy behavior to missing-cost hydration.

## Redacted behavior probe

Temporary state-dir probe with redacted transcript content and DeepSeek V4 catalog pricing:

```json
{
  "probe": "redacted DeepSeek V4 recorded-zero cost recompute",
  "inputTokens": 10000,
  "outputTokens": 10000,
  "cacheReadTokens": 10000,
  "recordedCostTotal": 0,
  "catalogCostPerMillion": {
    "input": 0.14,
    "output": 0.28,
    "cacheRead": 0.028,
    "cacheWrite": 0
  },
  "totalTokens": 30000,
  "totalCost": 0.00448,
  "sessionLog": {
    "tokens": 30000,
    "cost": 0.00448
  }
}
```

sessionLog cost 0.00448.

## Current head key Actions

- Recompute recorded-zero session transcript costs when the model has positive configured/catalog pricing and nonzero tokens.
- Clear stale zero breakdowns so spend summary and session log cost both use the recomputed estimate.
- Bump the usage-cost cache semantic version from 4 to 5 so old zero-preserving cache entries are rebuilt.
- Hydrate missing DeepSeek configured costs from bundled catalog pricing.
- Preserve explicit all-zero DeepSeek configured cost overrides.
- Keep `CHANGELOG.md` unchanged relative to `upstream/main`.

## Evidence

- `node --import tsx` redacted behavior probe: `"totalCost": 0.00448`; sessionLog cost 0.00448.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `git diff --check` passed locally on the PR branch.
- `CHANGELOG.md` has no diff relative to `upstream/main`.
- Added fixture coverage in `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` for known-priced DeepSeek V4 recorded zero cost, including summary totals, cache upgrade behavior, and `loadSessionLogs()` cost display.
- Added fixture coverage in `extensions/deepseek/provider-policy-api.test.ts` for missing-cost catalog hydration and explicit all-zero cost override preservation.

## Actions status

Current head `dd8cd0f`:

- Real behavior proof: success.
- `check-test-types`: success.
- QA Smoke CI: success.
- `check-docs`: skipped.
- `build-artifacts`: success.
- `check-lint`: success.
- `check-prod-types`: success.
- `check-shrinkwrap`: success.
- `check-dependencies`: success.
- `check-guards`: success.
